canonical_value: When \(x(t)\) is the input to a linear, time-invariant system with system function \(H(s)\) shown in (a), the output \(y(t)\) is the sum of a constant \(\beta\) plus a periodic train of impulses with area 1,
surface_forms: When \(x(t)\) is the input to a linear, time-invariant system with system function \(H(s)\) shown in (a), the output \(y(t)\) is the sum of a constant \(\beta\) plus a periodic train of impulses with area 1,

normalized_value.text: When \(x(t)\) is the input to a linear, time-invariant system with system function \(H(s)\) shown in (a), the output \(y(t)\) is the sum of a constant \(\beta\) plus a periodic train of impulses with area 1,
y(t) = \beta + \sum_{k=-\infty}^{\infty} \delta(t - 8k)

canonical_value: Consider the output of the same system if the period of the input signal is changed to \(T = 4\) seconds while keeping the Fourier series coefficients \(a_k\) unchanged.
surface_forms: Consider the output of the same system if the period of the input signal is changed to \(T = 4\) seconds while keeping the Fourier series coefficients \(a_k\) unchanged.

normalized_value.text: Consider the output of the same system if the period of the input signal is changed to \(T = 4\) seconds while keeping the Fourier series coefficients \(a_k\) unchanged.

canonical_value: Is it possible to determine the new output signal from the information provided?
surface_forms: Is it possible to determine the new output signal from the information provided?

normalized_value.target_text: Is it possible to determine the new output signal from the information provided?

canonical_value:
Let \(x(t)\) represent a periodic signal with period \(T = 8\) seconds, whose Fourier series coefficients are
\[
a_k = \begin{cases}
\frac{1}{j\pi k} & \text{for integers } k \neq 0 \\
0 & k = 0
\end{cases}
\]
When \(x(t)\) is the input to a linear, time-invariant system with system function \(H(s)\) shown in (a), the output \(y(t)\) is the sum of a constant \(\beta\) plus a periodic train of impulses with area 1,
\[
y(t) = \beta + \sum_{k=-\infty}^{\infty} \delta(t - 8k)
\]
as shown in (b).
Consider the output of the same system if the period of the input signal is changed to \(T = 4\) seconds while keeping the Fourier series coefficients \(a_k\) unchanged.
Is it possible to determine the new output signal from the information provided? Answer yes or no.
surface_forms:
Let \(x(t)\) represent a periodic signal with period \(T = 8\) seconds, whose Fourier series coefficients are
\[
a_k = \begin{cases}
\frac{1}{j\pi k} & \text{for integers } k \neq 0 \\
0 & k = 0
\end{cases}
\]
When \(x(t)\) is the input to a linear, time-invariant system with system function \(H(s)\) shown in (a), the output \(y(t)\) is the sum of a constant \(\beta\) plus a periodic train of impulses with area 1,
\[
y(t) = \beta + \sum_{k=-\infty}^{\infty} \delta(t - 8k)
\]
as shown in (b).
Consider the output of the same system if the period of the input signal is changed to \(T = 4\) seconds while keeping the Fourier series coefficients \(a_k\) unchanged.
Is it possible to determine the new output signal from the information provided? Answer yes or no.

normalized_value.title: EEE-Bench 开放题
normalized_value.rewritten_question_text:
Let \(x(t)\) represent a periodic signal with period \(T = 8\) seconds, whose Fourier series coefficients are
\[
a_k = \begin{cases}
\frac{1}{j\pi k} & \text{for integers } k \neq 0 \\
0 & k = 0
\end{cases}
\]
When \(x(t)\) is the input to a linear, time-invariant system with system function \(H(s)\) shown in (a), the output \(y(t)\) is the sum of a constant \(\beta\) plus a periodic train of impulses with area 1,
\[
y(t) = \beta + \sum_{k=-\infty}^{\infty} \delta(t - 8k)
\]
as shown in (b).
Consider the output of the same system if the period of the input signal is changed to \(T = 4\) seconds while keeping the Fourier series coefficients \(a_k\) unchanged.
Is it possible to determine the new output signal from the information provided? Answer yes or no.

canonical_value:
The state space equation of a system is described by
\[
\dot{x} = Ax + Bu, \quad y = Cx
\]
where \(x\) is the state vector, \(u\) is the input, \(y\) is the output, and
\[
A = \begin{bmatrix} 0 & 1 \\ 0 & -2 \end{bmatrix}, \quad B = \begin{bmatrix} 0 \\ 1 \end{bmatrix}, \quad C = [1 \quad 0]
\]
A unity feedback is provided to the above system \(G(s)\) to make it a closed-loop system.

For a unit step input \(r(t)\), what is the steady-state error?
